description: Promotional posts for food products and dishes that highlight menu items through appetizing visuals paired with colloquial Arabic captions emphasizing taste, tradition, and emotional connection to food.
structural_recipe: Feature a high-quality food product image (typically meat or prepared dish in a box/plating). Pair with conversational Arabic caption using colloquial dialect that emphasizes: (1) emotional benefit or mood elevation, (2) traditional/heritage preparation methods, (3) convenience of delivery/availability, (4) sensory language (taste descriptors like 'يولّع' - ignites). Include branded hashtags. Use emojis sparingly but meaningfully (fire, heart, meat icons). End with restaurant/brand hashtag.
why_it_works: Saudi audiences respond to food content that balances modern convenience with cultural authenticity. Captions that reference 'grandmothers' cooking methods' and 'no need to travel' tap into nostalgia while emphasizing accessibility. Emotional language ('يسعد الكل' - makes everyone happy, 'قلبك حب' - your heart loves) creates personal connection. The casual, intimate tone mirrors friend recommendations rather than corporate advertising.
transplantation_caveats: Colloquial Saudi/Gulf dialect is essential—formal Arabic performs poorly
Must respect halal and Islamic dietary sensitivities—pork and alcohol references are prohibited
Gender-mixed dining references should align with Saudi social norms
Pricing/luxury messaging must balance aspiration with perceived value
Time-sensitive posts perform better during meal hours (lunch 12-2pm, dinner 7-9pm local time)
cultural_constraints: Must use halal-certified or clearly Islamic-compliant messaging
Avoid imagery that conflicts with Islamic values
Family/social dining framing preferred over individual indulgence
Heritage and tradition references strengthen resonance
